title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-0 loss to Nashville SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution's seven-game unbeaten streak ended in a 3-0 loss to Nashville SC at Gillette Stadium.
Goalkeeper Matt Turner's mistake contributed to Nashville's 2-0 first-half lead, furthering New England's struggles.
Revolution fell to third in the Eastern Conference; upcoming match against Minnesota United poses another challenge.

title: Massport to attempt Guinness World Record for world’s largest soccer ball
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Massport will unveil a 45-foot-tall soccer ball at Piers Park II to attempt a Guinness World Record.
The current largest soccer ball record, set in Doha, Qatar in 2013, measures 38 feet and 11.8 inches.
A Community Day event with games and activities is planned for June 13 at the site in East Boston.

title: Famous makeup artist gets backlash for mocking former Spirit Airlines employee at Logan over fundraiser
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: James Charles faced backlash for mocking former Spirit Airlines employee Amber Lendof Vargas over a GoFundMe campaign.
Charles apologized and donated $5,000 to Vargas's fundraiser after calling her request "entitled" and "lazy" in a now-deleted video.
Spirit Airlines' sudden shutdown left about 17,000 employees jobless, including Vargas, who sought $35,000 via online fundraising.

title: Photos: State trooper killed by wrong-way driver in Lynnfield crash laid to rest
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Trooper Kevin Trainor, 30, was killed by a wrong-way driver in Lynnfield on May 6.
Funeral services held for Trooper Trainor at St. James Church in Salem on May 13.
Governor Maura Healey attended and spoke at the funeral service for Trooper Trainor.

title: Angell Animal Medical Center treating cat who fell 10 stories
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A cat named Mazy is being treated at Angell Animal Medical Center after falling nearly ten stories.
Mazy has broken paws and a collapsed lung, with surgery expected to cost up to $15,000.
Law enforcement is investigating the cause of Mazy's fall from a Greater Boston apartment building.

title: AG says auditor can hire attorney to force audit of Legislature after SJC order
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Attorney General Andrea Campbell allows Auditor Diana DiZoglio to hire an attorney for her legislative audit pursuit.
Massachusetts' Supreme Judicial Court set a 30-day deadline for AG's decision on the auditor's representation request.
Auditor DiZoglio's audit of the Legislature focuses on four Senate finance areas from fiscal years 2021 to 2024.

title: Kyle Schwarber reveals tepid pursuit from Red Sox this past offseason
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kyle Schwarber revealed the Red Sox made minimal efforts to re-sign him before he returned to the Phillies.
Since joining the Phillies, Schwarber has hit 204 home runs, showcasing his power despite a high strikeout rate.
Schwarber has a career OPS of 1.137 at Fenway Park, one of the highest for players with 100+ plate appearances.

title: Jayson Tatum reveals new goal he’s chasing with the Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum aims to win another NBA championship and secure Finals MVP with the Boston Celtics in the 2026-27 season.
Tatum is preparing for the upcoming season after recovering from a ruptured Achilles tendon that affected his 2025-26 performance.
Despite knee soreness in playoffs, Tatum expressed confidence in his ability to return to high-level play post-injury.

title: 7 contracts Bruins might try to unload this offseason
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins may trade Joonas Korpisalo to free up cap space; his contract has a $3 million cap hit.
The Bruins consider offloading Henri Jokiharju's $3 million contract for inconsistent performance, freeing a possible third-pair defense slot.
Boston faces challenges unloading Elias Lindholm's $7.75 million contract due to injury concerns and a no-movement clause.

title: First white shark sighting of 2026 confirmed off Massachusetts coast
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: First white shark of 2026 confirmed in Massachusetts waters after gray seal found dead on Martha's Vineyard.
Aquarium scientist John Chisholm verified seal's wound as a shark bite, marking the start of shark season.
Public urged to report shark sightings via the Sharktivity app for safety and scientific research purposes.
